title: COVID-19 Extraction-Free Direct One-Step Fast Cycling Protocol
author: steve.puts
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bkjpkumn

steps.instruction: Place the swab brush into a 1.5ml microcentrifuge tube containing 135 µL, PCR-grade water and 15 µL, PBS (10x conc) . Rotate the brush 5-10 times. Squeeze the brush and remove it from the tube.
Centrifuge at 12000 x g, °C, for 3 min at room temperature
Discard the supernatant. Add 4.5 µL, PCR-grade water and 0.5 µL, Extraction Buffer Briefly flick the sample.
Incubate for 3 min at room temperature for lysis and release of RNA.
Centrifuge briefly and transfer 0.25 µL, sample to the well plate. Pipette up/down to make sure the sample has fully released from the pipette.
Cover the sample with 1 µL, mineral oil
Load the well plate into the cycler and move the chamber door down. Click run. Run with parameters: - RT 50C for 3 min - Denaturation 95C for 0s (maximum speed) - Annealing 60C for 0s (maximum speed)
Pre-mix the reagents for 81 wells 81 µL, total volume 40.5 µL, Direct reaction mix 3.25 µL, Enzyme mix 8.25 µL, Primers and probes mix 29 µL, PCR grade water

substeps.instruction: Pre-mix the reagents for 81 wells 81 µL, total volume
µL, Primers and probes mix 29 µL, PCR grade water

steps.instruction: Pipette the mix onto the 81 well plate. To gain speed, use a pipette-ing robot.
